title: Charred Pillowy Moroccan Beef Flank Steak Tagine
description: A romantic Moroccan dish featuring charred beef flank steak, en-croute, in a flavorful tagine sauce.
instructions: Season the beef flank steak with salt, cayenne pepper, and black pepper.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, egg, and a pinch of salt. Add the beef flank steak and coat evenly.
Heat the olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the beef flank steak for 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the beef reaches your desired level of doneness.
While the beef cooks, prepare the tagine sauce by sautéing the chopped onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger in a little oil until softened.
Add the cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Once the beef is cooked, remove it from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes.
To assemble the tagine, place the beef flank steak in the center of a large ramekin or baking dish.
Pour the tagine sauce over the beef, then sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
Cover the ramekin with foil and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the sauce is bubbly and the beef is tender.
Serve the charred pillowy Moroccan beef flank steak tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
